title: Pemphigus Vulgaris
chief_complaint: I have painful blisters and sores in my mouth for 2 months. Now they're spreading to my skin. The blisters break easily and leave raw, weeping areas. It hurts to eat and drink. I've lost 20 pounds because I can't swallow.

physical_exam.Oral: Multiple painful erosions on buccal mucosa, hard palate, and gingiva. Nikolsky sign positive (rubbing normal-appearing mucosa causes blistering).
physical_exam.Skin: Flaccid bullae on chest, back, and scalp. Multiple erosions with crusting. Nikolsky sign positive on skin (lateral pressure on normal skin causes shearing).

hidden_data.skin_biopsy: Suprabasal acantholysis with tombstone pattern of basal keratinocytes. Intraepidermal blister.
hidden_data.direct_immunofluorescence: IgG and C3 deposited in intercellular spaces throughout epidermis (fishnet pattern). Diagnostic for pemphigus.
hidden_data.indirect_immunofluorescence: Serum antibodies against intercellular substance (desmoglein). Titer 1:640.
hidden_data.elisa: Anti-desmoglein 3 antibodies: Positive (mucosal dominant). Anti-desmoglein 1 antibodies: Weakly positive.
hidden_data.cmp: Albumin 2.5 (malnutrition from oral ulcers), normal creatinine

key_learning_points: Pemphigus vulgaris = autoimmune blistering disease targeting desmoglein 3 (mucosal) and desmoglein 1 (skin). IgG autoantibodies against desmosomes → acantholysis (loss of keratinocyte adhesion)
Nikolsky sign = shearing of epidermis with lateral pressure (positive in pemphigus, toxic epidermal necrolysis, SSSS). Different from Nikolsky in bullous pemphigoid (NEGATIVE - subepidermal blister)
Pemphigus: Flaccid bullae that rupture easily → painful erosions. Bullous pemphigoid: Tense bullae on flexural surfaces, pruritic, less mucosal involvement
Oral involvement in 70-90% (often first manifestation). Potentially fatal without treatment (fluid loss, infection, malnutrition)

Treatment: Prednisone 1mg/kg/day (mainstay). Rituximab (anti-CD20) is first-line steroid-sparing agent. Azathioprine or Mycophenolate for maintenance. IVIG for refractory. Monitor weight, BP, glucose, bone density. Slow taper over months. Mucosal lesions heal last
